The Team

Eight Engineers.
One Mission.

Team Vulcan is the institutional successor to Team Jabari, who placed 3rd overall and won the Best Creativity Award at Cars4Mars 2025. We are a multi-disciplinary team from Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology, building on every lesson from 2025 to field the most capable Kenyan rover ever entered.

Victoria Rotich
Telecomms & Information Engineering
Telemetry, Robot modelling & simulation, ROS architecture oversight
Shadrack Kinsimba
Electrical & Computer Engineering
AI Systems, Computer Vision, RTOS
Brian Fundi
Mechatronics Engineering
CAD Design, Robotic Control Frameworks
John Sumba
Mechanical Engineering
3D Modelling, Fabrication, Simulation
Shalom Kiptanui
Mechatronics Engineering
Robotic Arm, Path Planning
Collins Kiprop
Aerospace Engineering
FEA Simulation, Composite Materials
Allen Wachio
Mechatronics Engineering
Electronics, Custom PCB Design (KiCad)
Joan Ouma
Electrical & Computer Engineering
IoT Architectures, ESP32/Arduino
The Rover

Project Vulcan —
Edge AI on Red Dirt

What you see here is our test platform, not the final build — a working foundation we're actively iterating on, complete with views of our fast, simplified 3D ROS robot model in the carousel. But the core innovation is already proven: full onboard edge AI, running vision, path planning, and sensor fusion entirely locally — no cloud, no lag. The same architecture challenge faced in real Mars exploration, solved at student scale, in Kenya.

VULCAN PROTOTYPE — Field Test, JKUAT Campus 01 / 06
AI Compute NVIDIA Jetson platform — high TOPS on-device
Vision Real-time object detection @ high fps
Localization Multi-sensor fusion
Motor Control High-frequency closed-loop control with encoder feedback
LiDAR 3D scene mapping + 1D fast object tracking
Motor Drivers Compact high-efficiency MOSFET drivers
Battery High-density Li-Po pack — fast-charge capable
Software Stack ROS2 + Ubuntu
RF RF-optimized enclosure with high-gain antenna
The Competition

Cars4Mars African
Rover Challenge

The Cars4Mars African Rover Challenge is the premier student robotics and space technology competition in Africa and the MENA region. University teams design, build, and operate a mobile Mars rover prototype — controlled wirelessly and powered entirely by an onboard battery — across two high-stakes phases.

Scoring Missions

Traversal Mission
Manually driven run over Martian terrain — slopes, rocks, obstacles
250 pts
Autonomous Mission
Rover uses on-board AI to detect objects and navigate without human input
150 pts
Total: 400+ points available

Mars Stage Final

Date 19 September 2026
Location SANSA Space Operations Facility, Hartebeesthoek, Johannesburg, South Africa
Format Technical missions simulating Martian field conditions
Teams Top-qualifying African university teams from the Launch Stage (online phase)
Predecessor Team Jabari — 3rd Place + Best Creativity Award, Cars4Mars 2025
31 May 2026
Project Strategy Report Online
Initial rover design and team execution strategy submitted to international judges. Up to 50 points.
2 Aug 2026
Video + Final Design Report Online
A five-minute video demonstrating remote driving, wireless streaming, and autonomous computer vision capabilities. Up to 250 points.
22 Aug 2026
Launch Stage Final Online
Live presentation to space industry judges covering project management and commercial potential. Up to 100 points.
19 Sep 2026
Mars Stage — In-Person Final Johannesburg
Teams compete at the SANSA Space Operations facility. The Traversal Mission (250 pts) tests terrain navigation; the Autonomous Mission (150 pts) requires the rover to independently identify objects and navigate to targets without any human input.
Budget & Bill of Materials

Where Every
Dollar Goes

Transparent, milestone-based funding. Every component is documented, every vendor verified.

A Milestone A
The Rover
KES 197K
Rover equipment only. Minimum viable hardware to compete.
B Milestone B — Guaranteed
Rover Ready
KES 697K
Full rover build + travel for 2 core developers. Competition attendance guaranteed.
C Milestone C
Expanded Squad
KES 1.35M
Full rover build + travel for 5 squad members.
D Milestone D — Full Goal
Full Team
KES 2.43M
Full build + travel for the entire team, plus transport, meals, accommodation, and contingency in Johannesburg. Maximum competition presence.
